I am extremely disappointed with the service I received from this attorney. My experience was nothing short of a nightmare. From the outset, it was clear that professionalism and competence were not priorities for this attorney. I wish I could find these reviews before signing a contract with these people.

First, communication was abysmal. Despite numerous attempts to get clear and advanced updates on my case, my calls and emails were often ignored or answered with a vague and unhelpful response. The LACK OF TRANSPARENCY and accountability was incredibly frustrating and left me in the dark for 7 months.

Additionally, the first attorney (I wasn't aware was someone different from Mr. Barina) seemed completely disorganized and unprepared. 5+ months later after I signed a contract with the attorney, I found out that the attorney running my case was no longer working in the office and whoever would be in charge of my case needed time to catch up on the case. Important deadlines were missed, and crucial documents were incorrectly filed. Their blatant disregard for detail and lack of diligence was appalling and severely jeopardized the outcome of my case.

Overall, Bobby Dale Barina's office provided the worst legal service I ever experienced. Their unprofessionalism, incompetence, and lack of basic communication skills made an already stressful situation infinitely worse and incomplete. Hours before I got on a plane for military purposes, I found out that they were unwilling to represent me for my case due "absence of jurisdiction." Information that, they probably knew, for a long time ago and they held on shared with me.  7 months after I am in the same place where I started with the case, with $5,000 less in my bank account, and waiting to finish with my military obligations to go back to the States and restart the process due to their hiding of information. I WOULD STRONGLY ADVISE ANYONE CONSIDERING THIS FIRM TO LOOK ELSEWHERE FOR LEGAL REPRESENTATION.

P.S. Incredibly diligent in replying to a bad review and remembering a client. For future reference, next time I suggest providing straightforward and consistent information from the beginning. This will ensure that new clients have clear and realistic expectations. This is crucial for foresting trust and confidence between both parties. CLEARLY, you didn't complete the task. As a result, of your performance, it hurts my trust in your competence and services.

I absolutely would not recommend this place for a divorce lawyer.

The lawyer I had, hardly did one thing for me. She completely wasted my time.

She would not speak to me in laymens terms even after I would ask for clarification, she seemed to get annoyed that I didn't want her to use her lawyer jargon.

She seemingly wanted to believe my ex over me and I was the one that hired this firm...not my ex! They did more work for my ex than they did for me and charged ME for it, not my ex.

They did not make me feel like I was a valued client.

They racked up my bill-they charged me 10k to do a temporary TRO (which did nothing for me because my ex was still able to do as he pleased with no consequences). And every time my ex would lie, I had to prove that lie wrong, and all the law firm did was make paper copies of each and every lie that my ex would tell them and charge me for copies. Nothing came of the lies in court. My ex was able to keep telling the lies even though I had proof. My lawyer never defended me from my ex to the Judge.

I really don't foresee copies of paper work that I already gave them (and I did all the work for the law firm) and a Temporary TRO is $10k. After I started to see that we were not moving forward in court and wasting my time, I started to get upset, naturally. Which in turn upset my lawyer (it seemed) and she quit on me saying that we aren't able to communicate. I already told this company from the get go that I do not have money to blow stupidly and I am on a VERY low income and my ex was taking EVERYTHING from me little by little..... he is an abusive manipulative lazy bum.. That didn't seem to matter to them what so ever.

My lawyer quit on me in the middle of my divorce process. That was actually a Godsend because I was able to hire another lawyer out of a different law office (NOT Carlson Law Firm) and that lawyer was VERY reasonably priced, got the things done over YEARS of sticking with me and never quit on me, never charged me an arm and a leg over the years it took to finalize the divorce, spoke to me like I was a valued client, and was very nice through and through.

I would NOT recommend Carlson Law Firm for divorce lawyers.

reviews.text:
I am writing to formally express my deep dissatisfaction and outrage regarding the representation provided by court-appointed attorney Michael [Last Name], who was assigned to defend my nephew in a serious murder case. From the beginning, Mr. Michael demonstrated a lack of commitment, professionalism, and care.

He advised us that a bond hearing would be a “waste of time” because he assumed the family could not afford bail. This was not only presumptive but offensive. In fact, our family managed to raise $50,000 to hire a qualified attorney who is now fighting for my nephew’s rights—something Mr. Michael failed to do.

Mr. Michael pressured my nephew to accept a 30-year plea deal without presenting us with any strategic defense or alternative options. He appeared more interested in quickly closing the case than in advocating for justice. His actions felt like an intentional attempt to railroad my nephew into a plea—despite not knowing the full facts or circumstances.

To make matters worse, he blocked my nephew’s sister from checking on the status of the case, cutting off family communication and transparency. His behavior was not only unprofessional but deeply harmful.

Based on his actions, I believe Mr. Michael is not acting in the best interest of his clients, particularly young Black men. It is my opinion that he is colluding with the prosecution and that his conduct reflects serious racial bias and ethical concerns.

I am grateful we were able to retain a real attorney who cares. But I remain concerned for other families who may not be able to do the same. No family should have to fight both the legal system and their own court-appointed counsel.

I respectfully ask that this complaint be investigated and that appropriate actions be taken.

I have been back and forth in prayer concerning what I am about to post. This is not a negative review more of a warning to future clients and hoping this will change Mr. White's mindset towards his future clients (wrong or right) that the court appoint him as their public defender.

It is never acceptable for a public defender to tell his client's mother, "That's why your son is in jail and not me!" (in the courthouse entrance loud enough that others could hear that was there) At any given time this comment is not acceptable about your client that you are representing. I'm torn as a mother by the many other words that he said, and it spoke volume to me about what type of attorney my son currently have representing him. He is clearly over looking the obvious...

Leaving this review might make it worse for my son, because Mr. White is still handling his case at the moment. (My trust is in God to show what is really going on that is being over looked) As I was led to do this by the Holy Spirit, because Mr. White thinks what he said was right and didn't apologize about what he said after I apologized to him for my outburst after I told him that I didn't want to talk to him and I needed to calm down because I was upset in which I wasn't upset because of him. It was the whole situation... but Mr. White didn't respect my wishes and kept explaining to me the procedures. What was playing over and over in my head at the moment was how the prosecutor described my son. I was angry hurt upset feeling guilty and all other things was going on in my mind. (I got it that's the prosecutor's job, but as a parent I still have the right to be emotional, what mother wants to see their child go to jail and to hear someone paint her child as a monster and you know truly is not. I was really trying not to react out of emotions) Since he kept talking, I spoke in a loud voice, "You don't know my son. I live with him every day..." I took what the prosecutor said out on him because of what was rehearsing in my mind at the time.

After my apology and honestly explaining to Mr. White through text how his word effected me whether my son was wrong or right as his defender he shouldn't have that type of mindset towards his own client. It clearing shows by his comments that he not for my son. He never apologized nor tried to make me feel any different towards him as my son's public defender. He seemed to careless. In His eyes, my son was wrong bottom-line.

Hiring James Tisdale was one of the biggest mistakes I made during my divorce. I retained him to represent me in a high-conflict case, and from the start, there were red flags — poor communication, delays, and what appeared to be a lack of genuine advocacy on my behalf.

For example, after he officially withdrew from my case in March 2025, he continued to bill me for communications and administrative tasks — some of which never occurred. It wasn't until I challenged the invoice that over $1,200 in charges were suddenly removed, without explanation. That alone raises serious questions about the accuracy and honesty of his billing practices.

More troubling, he failed to provide critical case materials, including emails with opposing counsel that I specifically and repeatedly requested in writing, over the phone, and in person. These communications were my legal property under Texas Disciplinary Rule 1.15(d), and yet I was never given full access to them.

On top of that, Mr. Tisdale made damaging statements in official correspondence — including suggesting I had anger issues — despite missing a potential court notice requesting a psychological evaluation. His oversight and lack of diligence could have caused serious harm to my case.

I would not recommend James Tisdale to anyone seeking competent, ethical, and responsive legal representation. Proceed with caution.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Miller was a client and is a prime example of why you should always follow your attorney’s advice.
I recommended we immediately set her case for temporary orders. We filed the proper paperwork and had a hearing scheduled, but before we could get into court, Ms. Miller said that she and her husband had an agreement and didn’t need a hearing. I advised her to keep the hearing, because until the agreement was made into an order, it wouldn’t be enforceable. Still, Ms. Miller insisted that there was no need for a hearing with the agreement they had and told my office to cancel the hearing. We relented and cancelled the hearing with no order in place.
Months later, our client complains that her husband isn't following the agreement. We tell her that we need to schedule a temporary hearing because we have nothing enforceable in place. By this point she has run out of money in trust. We asked consistently for her to supplement her trust so we could work on her case and we never heard from her, so we were forced to withdraw.
Moral of the story: you hire your attorney for a reason. Let him do his job and follow his advice. Don’t trust the person you are divorcing more than the person hired to represent you.

reviews.text:
I can honestly say this case started off rough. I was arrested during a traffic stop because of a warrant from when I was 16 years old — I’m now 39 — and I had no idea it even existed. My wife and I live in California and were just traveling through Texas when this happened.

Because the case was so old, it came with a lot of complications. We had to travel back and forth to Texas multiple times for court, which was expensive and stressful. Court dates were sometimes canceled last minute (not the attorneys’ fault), and we quickly learned to book refundable flights.

Through it all, this legal team stood by us.

The husband-and-wife attorneys who handled my case were not only knowledgeable, but genuinely kind people. They treated us with respect and never made us feel like just another file. From day one, they pushed to have the case closed due to the age of the case and lack of evidence or witnesses.

Their communication was consistent and clear. We always knew what was happening. The office staff and secretaries were also incredible — patient, organized, and extremely helpful when it came to scheduling and coordinating around our travel from out of state. That made a huge difference for us.

There were even circumstances outside anyone’s control — including a government shutdown that caused flight cancellations — and they worked with the court to make sure I could appear by Zoom so the case could continue moving forward.

In the end, we got the result we were hoping for.

The process wasn’t easy, but having a team that genuinely cared, communicated well, and kept fighting for me made all the difference. I’m very grateful for their dedication and professionalism.

Throughout the last 12 months, I have been through the most humiliating, exhausting & demeaning time in my life. Sounds dramatic, but it’s absolutely true.

Melissa & Zach, though, stood by me from the beginning. They helped me. They counseled me. They gave me their expert (and accurate) opinion on how to deal with the situation... but above all, it is their passion and willingness to do whatever it takes in order to protect and defend their clients, allowing them to keep their dignity and pride.

I know, without a doubt, that If I had gone with another lawyer who wasn’t so familiar with Bell Co & who could see things in a different light than what they were presented... my positive outcome would have not been so positive, I can honestly say that because originally I had a different lawyer - and I felt alone, in the middle of the ocean with no life jacket. No positivity, no support.... it was bad.

I used to not have the highest opinion of defense lawyers (thank you, TV & really bad commercials), but Melissa & Zach showed me how wrong I was & really showed me that they truly care for my well-being & future. It was shown through their help throughout this situation, through the ups and downs, even talking me through some hysterical phone calls I’m not very proud of.

Frankly, they probably saved my life more than once, when I was dealing with everything and found myself thrown into the midst of my darkest moments. They let me know time and time again, I wasn’t alone, no matter how it felt. Love them both & I will never be able to thank them enough.

reviews.text: Overall a terrible experience. I was charged multiple times for the same thing, mislead about the time frame of my case, and the costs. At the start of my case I signed up for my services, and was told my retainer should cover everything, and we would be done in 6 to 8 months. During the middle of my case things changed, and I required less services, however the cost exceeded even the 1st estimate that included more work. My case would be put off for months, the turnover in the office was disturbing. I had several people toss my case around to each other. And when it came time to wrap up my case, I was treated disrespectfully because I had several questions. It got to the point where I wanted to just leave and no longer have them represent me. I had to actually speak to the main lawyer to resolve and move forward. But not only did Thomas lie to me about the status of my attorney, telling me she was just a billing employee... Only to have her show up and represent me in court. I was mislead. Ending my services was one of the best things I could have done for my stress, and I regret not following my instincts. We paid our final bill, and expected to hear nothing else. I just received another bill with no follow up. Communication isn't this company's strong suit. I would not recommend.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I am disappointed to read this comment. We strive very hard to make every client happy, but given the sheer numbers of clients we represent, we occasionally fall short of that goal. We believe your intentional omission of your name is telling, but since we have very few unhappy clients we can guess who you are anyway. The inaccuracies of your statement continue from there, so we will address a few of them.
We do not offer flat fees. Therefore every client is told verbally and in writing that we cannot estimate total fees. You were told the same, and we have copied the following from your initial email.
“We will accept a $xxx advance in the hope this case doesn't require protracted litigation. Please keep in mind the total cost could, and likely will, easily exceed the initial retainer if you have multiple hearings, depositions, discovery, temporary order hearings, appointments, revisions, etc. That amount is simply an advance payment to allow us to begin working the case. We can never anticipate a "total" cost because it is dependent on so many factors - the primary one being how the opposing party ultimately litigates the case. You could get money back or you could pay more. It is completely dependent on the time expended on your case.”
Second, your case was not delayed due to apathy. We have all had to bear the burden of a national pandemic. The Courts are very backed up and will be for some time. Settings are hard to get, and when litigants fail to reach any amicable agreements, then they must wait for the Court’s availability to make a rulings on each disputed issue. That increases both legal costs and the time it takes to conclude a case. Further, the billing related to multiple hearings may appear to some to be the same thing billed twice, but in actuality the same tasks are having to be done more than once. For example, each hearing requires pleadings, subpoenas, preparation time, and notices of the hearing. It is not the same work, but rather similar work for additional steps.
The assertion that someone lied to you about being a lawyer is as ridiculous as it is ignorant. You should probably consider the fact that in a Firm with six offices and dozens of employees, some employees may have the same name.
Finally, when someone abuses our staff, staff are given the option to respectfully defend themselves. No employee here is required to listen to degrading personal comments simply because they are employed here.
We are thankful for the hundreds of people we have represented who have been happy with our services. We are thankful for the few people who have been patient when we have made a mistake or error and let us fix it to ensure they were happy in the end. We have learned greatly from them. Then, there are a minor few that we are glad for our own stress level that they have moved on. As mentioned, “it was the best thing that could have happened” for us. For what it is worth, we want all clients to be happy, so we are disappointed that you were not. We will try to learn from your experience to be better for the next client.

reviews.text: I had a consultation with Richard. Payment link for consult was sent to me to which I gladly paid.  Payment process and scheduling of consultation was seamless. Proceeding beyond that was difficult. After the consultation, the communication was lacking. Richard informed me that I would be charged again  to proceed. I agreed however the link was never sent as it was the first time. There were no different instructions provided on where/how to submit payment information. I patiently waited and called numerous times. No calls were returned for months eliminating the option to even pay via phone. Firm stated that I refused to pay and that several months passed without hearing from me when in fact I did attempt to contact them several times and they failed to respond.  After I left a review in March, they promptly respond to my email from January demonstrating that they had the capability to respond to clarify anything or send the payment link/payment info all along.  They asked me to remove my review in exchange for a refund. The firm is tricky their wording/responses and refuse to acknowledge that they went months without returning my email and phone calls emphasizing that no payment was made (although I attempted contact them via phone to inquire about why I received a payment link for the first payment, but not for the fee needed to continue).  The firm places blame on customers lacking the integrity to admit their short comings. From other reviews, its becoming obvious that they are not always responsive.  Its ironic that they were able to promptly respond to a negative review almost immediately, but take months to respond to an email or phone call. They have made the issue out to be that they could not proceed because I did not pay. The simple truth is that I was forgotten about for months until I left a review. Payment link could have been sent at any time just like it was for the consultation.

I highly recommend this law office. Jordan Hutchins was very professional, knowledgeable, and personable.  She answered my questions and explained to me everything I needed to know about my case. Communcation was prompt, and my case was handled in a timely manner. Thank you so much!

reviews.text: I made an appointment in late June and got an appointment for July 15. The front office was very polite and professional. He said he would consult with his colleagues to confirm the best course for me, and said it wouldn’t be a complicated matter. I never heard from him again. I followed up two weeks later via email, and in the next business week an admin called to relay a message from him. She was unable to answer my follow up questions, so I asked to have the lawyer return my call. It is now 3 months later. So my experience was pay a consultation fee that would be applied to services later and then no follow through on the requested services. I do not recommend this law firm, but I would definitely recommend whoever handles their SEO and marketing. They drive traffic and make conversion easy only for the legal team to disappoint.
